title: 'He changed in a very insidious way': Parents' distress at sons embracing toxic masculinity
subhed: The rise of masculinist rhetoric among young people is creating new tensions within families. Mothers and sisters are often targeted by boys who have been radicalized online. Fathers are left feeling helpless.

title: EU-Mercosur trade deal set to take effect without waiting for European Parliament vote
Sweden confirms that drone near French aircraft carrier was Russian
French court upholds 'far left' label for Mélenchon's LFI
subhed: The Council of State rejected the appeal filed by Jean-Luc Mélenchon's La France Insoumise against the Interior Ministry's decision to remove its candidates from the 'left bloc' for the upcoming municipal elections.

title: Paramount acquires Warner Bros. in $110 billion deal, creating Hollywood behemoth
subhed: The final deal caps a five-month bidding battle with Netflix, and will give birth to one of the most powerful entertainment conglomerates in the world.

title: Could Punch the monkey make us a little more human?
subhed: Social media users around the world are captivated by the plight of a baby monkey abandoned by its mother and taken in by a Japanese zoo. The story reveals how our tendency toward anthropomorphism can spark a rare, collective empathy.

title: 'The Louvre director's resignation raises an old question: Why do cultural execs go mad?'
subhed: Laurence des Cars, who handed her resignation from the Paris museum on February 24, joined a list of cultural executives accused of being overzealous. The French government is not without responsibility, analyzes Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin.

title: How Paris is turning into 'Frollywood,' a refuge for American actors
subhed: Whether George Clooney, Matt Dillon or Kristen Stewart, France is attracting more and more actors from across the Atlantic. This trend speaks to both the Trump-era crisis and the decline of the Hollywood model.
byline: By Laurent Carpentier and Aureliano Tonet

title: Ukraine: 'The war could still last for years and paradoxically, time is not on the Kremlin's side'
subhed: Researcher Dimitri Minic tells Le Monde in an interview that it is 'too soon to write off Ukraine,' even though the balance of power currently favors Russia.

title: 'The unnatural alliance between Trump and White evangelicals has cost them dearly, even if it allowed them to gain positions of power'
subhed: Historian Sébastien Fath discusses the history and dynamics of evangelical Protestant churches in an interview with Le Monde. Despite their current vibrancy, their growth is not inevitable, the researcher argues.

title: 'The Epstein scandal is astonishing by the scale of the complicity and rape culture at the top of society'
